category_title: Young child with severe autism exhibiting physical aggression to escape demands or gain attention
scenario_title: Snack time with restricted access to preferred food
minimal_context: Lauren is sitting next to her teacher in the classroom during snack time to eat her preferred food.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You can have this in a minute. (Places the preferred food item in sight but out of reach)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Screams loudly, pushes the table, and starts kicking and grabbing the teacher)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: It is time for snack.

STUDENT: Snack.

TUTOR: You can have this in a minute. (Places the preferred food item in sight but out of reach)

STUDENT: Eat!

TUTOR: We just need to wait.

STUDENT: (Screams loudly, pushes the table, and starts kicking and grabbing the teacher)

context.environment: Self-contained special education classroom for students with autism
context.social_setting: Regularly scheduled activities alongside her classmates; teacher sitting next to the participant

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Teacher placed the preferred food item in sight but out of the participant’s reach and told the participant, 'You can have this in a minute.'
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Screaming, pushing, kicking, and grabbing teachers
related_quotes: You can have this in a minute.

behavior_patterns.condition: When preferred items are placed in sight but out of reach
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Episodes of aggressive behaviors lasted up to 2 min
